workflow_name: Customer Inquiry Thread -> Response + Pipeline Note
user_message: Just came out of account health with Evergreen Solutions. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-16 14:50 EST
Subject: Re: account health Discussion - Next Steps

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with account health. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
Date: 2024-02-15 16:17 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Evergreen Solutions account health

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about account health. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-11 11:58 EST
Subject: Re: account health Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the account health proposal. Our IT Director is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Evergreen Solutions: there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope

handoffs.condition: MSX update warranted from thread content

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF33-P trap (Separation of follow-up draft from pipeline note forces context-switching); produces complete Thread analysis, draft response, waiting-on-whom assessment, MSX pipeline note proposal (if warrante
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF33-P failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF33-P failure mode (Separation of follow-up draft from pipeline note forces context-switching)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF33-P -- Separation of follow-up draft from pipeline note forces context-switching
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
